_comment: Knowledge source for the Data Visualization agent. Extracted from artifacts/insurance-dashboard/src/pages/VisualsCatalog.tsx (the CHARTS array). `dashboardChartType` maps each catalog entry to a chart type the dashboard renderer actually supports; `supported`=false entries have no renderable equivalent and must NOT be chosen by the agent (the nearest fallback is given for reference only). Supported dashboard chart types: bar, horizontal-bar, stacked-bar, line, area, pie, donut, scatter, bubble, combo, funnel, radar, treemap, histogram, bullet, waterfall, heatmap, progress-bar, gauge, kpi, table.

charts.name: Bar Chart
charts.whenToUse: Ranking or comparing 3-12 discrete items side-by-side with a single measure.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension (X) + 1 numeric measure (Y).
charts.bestFor: Monthly sales by region, revenue by product, headcount by department.
charts.avoidWhen: Continuous time-series (use line) or >15 categories.

charts.name: Horizontal Bar Chart
charts.whenToUse: Comparing 5-20 items where category names are long, or to show a clear ranking.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 1 numeric measure. Sort by value.
charts.bestFor: Top 10 customer segments, country revenue rankings, leaderboards.
charts.avoidWhen: Fewer than 4 items or data has a time component.

charts.name: Grouped Bar Chart
charts.whenToUse: Comparing 2-4 related measures across the same categories simultaneously.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 2-4 numeric measures (yKey as array).
charts.bestFor: Actual vs budget vs forecast, multi-year comparisons, A/B results.
charts.avoidWhen: More than 4 measures per group.

charts.name: Stacked Bar Chart
charts.whenToUse: When total size matters AND you want to show each category's contribution to the total.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 2-5 numeric sub-categories that stack to a total.
charts.bestFor: Revenue breakdown by product line per quarter, headcount by role per region.
charts.avoidWhen: Comparing individual segments across bars or >6 stack layers.

charts.name: 100% Stacked Bar
charts.whenToUse: When relative proportions (not absolute values) need comparing across groups.
charts.dataNeeded: Same as stacked bar; each bar normalized to 100%.
charts.bestFor: Market share over time, survey Likert results, budget allocation.
charts.avoidWhen: Absolute totals matter, or groups have similar composition.

charts.name: Line Chart
charts.whenToUse: Showing a metric's trend over time; ideal with >=6 equally-spaced periods.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 time dimension (X) + 1-4 numeric measures. Time must be continuous & ordered.
charts.bestFor: Monthly revenue, website traffic, KPI trend monitoring.
charts.avoidWhen: Categorical (non-time) X-axis or fewer than 5 points.

charts.name: Area Chart
charts.whenToUse: Like a line chart but when filled area adds meaningful context (volume, cumulative).
charts.dataNeeded: Same as line chart.
charts.bestFor: Cumulative revenue, user growth, inventory levels over time.
charts.avoidWhen: Multiple overlapping series.

charts.name: Stacked Area Chart
charts.whenToUse: Tracking how multiple categories contribute to a total over time.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 time dimension + 2-5 numeric measures forming a logical total.
charts.bestFor: Revenue by product line over time, traffic by source.
charts.avoidWhen: Comparing individual series values precisely.

charts.name: Combo Chart (Bar + Line)
charts.whenToUse: Comparing an absolute measure (bar) with a rate/ratio (line) of a different scale.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 time/category dimension + 1 bar measure + 1 line measure (dual Y-axes).
charts.bestFor: Revenue (bars) with growth rate (line), volume with avg deal size.
charts.avoidWhen: Both measures share the same scale or >2 measures.

charts.name: Step / Step-Line Chart
charts.whenToUse: When a value holds constant between change events (pricing, thresholds).
charts.dataNeeded: Same as line chart but discrete state that changes at specific moments.
charts.bestFor: Price changes, subscription tier upgrades, interest rate history.
charts.avoidWhen: Gradual continuous changes.

charts.name: Pie Chart
charts.whenToUse: Displaying a few (<=5) part-to-whole shares where relative size is the key message.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 1 numeric measure summing to a meaningful whole.
charts.bestFor: Budget allocation, market share among 3-5 brands, revenue split.
charts.avoidWhen: >5 slices, precise comparison needed, or values are very similar.

charts.name: Donut Chart
charts.whenToUse: Same as pie chart; the hollow center displays a key summary metric.
charts.dataNeeded: Same as pie chart.
charts.bestFor: Customer segment breakdown showing total count, budget with total spend.
charts.avoidWhen: >5 segments or the center value adds confusion.

charts.name: Treemap
charts.whenToUse: Part-to-whole relationships for many items (>6) in a space-efficient way.
charts.dataNeeded: 1-2 categorical dimensions (hierarchy) + 1 numeric measure for area.
charts.bestFor: Product category sales breakdown, portfolio allocation.
charts.avoidWhen: Precise comparison needed or fewer than 5 items.

charts.name: Radial Bar Chart
charts.whenToUse: Comparing progress/completion percentages for several categories compactly.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 1 numeric measure (percentage/ratio 0-100).
charts.bestFor: Multi-KPI performance, target achievement across departments.
charts.avoidWhen: Precise value comparison needed.

charts.name: Histogram
charts.whenToUse: Exploring how a continuous numeric variable is distributed across bins.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 continuous numeric variable; bin into ranges and count occurrences (use SQL width_bucket or CASE bins, then COUNT).
charts.bestFor: Customer age distribution, deal size frequency, delivery time spread.
charts.avoidWhen: Categorical data or fewer than 20 points.

charts.name: Scatter Plot
charts.whenToUse: Exploring whether two continuous variables are correlated, or finding outliers.
charts.dataNeeded: 2 continuous numeric variables (X and Y); each row is one observation.
charts.bestFor: Marketing spend vs revenue, price vs units sold.
charts.avoidWhen: One variable is categorical or fewer than 10 points.

charts.name: Bubble Chart
charts.whenToUse: Showing relationships between 3 continuous variables simultaneously.
charts.dataNeeded: 3 numeric variables: X, Y, and size (Z).
charts.bestFor: Product portfolio (x=growth, y=margin, size=revenue).
charts.avoidWhen: Precise size comparison needed or heavy overlap.

charts.name: Box & Whisker Plot
charts.whenToUse: Comparing statistical distributions (median, spread, outliers) across groups.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 1 continuous numeric variable, >=20 obs/group.
charts.bestFor: Salary distributions by department.
charts.avoidWhen: Audience unfamiliar with statistics.

charts.name: Funnel Chart
charts.whenToUse: Tracking how volume decreases across sequential stages in a pipeline.
charts.dataNeeded: Ordered stage names + numeric count/percentage per stage.
charts.bestFor: Sales pipeline (leads->close), website conversion funnel, onboarding steps.
charts.avoidWhen: Stages are not truly sequential.

charts.name: Waterfall Chart
charts.whenToUse: Explaining how an initial value is affected by a series of +/- changes.
charts.dataNeeded: Ordered labels with positive (increase) and negative (decrease) values.
charts.bestFor: P&L bridge, cash flow statement, variance analysis, budget to actuals.
charts.avoidWhen: Values don't logically accumulate, or >10 steps.

charts.name: Radar / Spider Chart
charts.whenToUse: Comparing an entity across 5-8 dimensions simultaneously.
charts.dataNeeded: 5-8 labeled axes + 1-3 numeric measures per axis (normalized 0-100).
charts.bestFor: Competitive benchmarking, performance reviews, feature comparisons.
charts.avoidWhen: >8 axes or very different scales without normalization.

charts.name: Slope Chart
charts.whenToUse: Highlighting which items increased/decreased between two specific points.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 1 numeric measure at exactly 2 time periods.
charts.bestFor: Before/after program impact, year-over-year change.
charts.avoidWhen: More than 2 time periods or >10 items.

charts.name: Heatmap
charts.whenToUse: Revealing patterns/concentrations across two categorical dimensions.
charts.dataNeeded: 2 categorical dimensions (rows & columns) + 1 numeric measure as cell color.
charts.bestFor: Engagement by day/time, correlation matrix, geo-demographic patterns.
charts.avoidWhen: Too few cells or exact values matter more than patterns.

charts.name: KPI Metric Cards
charts.whenToUse: Surfacing headline numbers with context (auto-generated for the dashboard; rarely emit manually).
charts.dataNeeded: Single aggregated numeric value per card.
charts.bestFor: Executive dashboards, management summaries.
charts.avoidWhen: Precise trends needed.

charts.name: Gauge / Dial Chart
charts.whenToUse: Communicating how a key metric performs relative to a target.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 numeric value + target value.
charts.bestFor: Sales quota achievement, NPS, SLA compliance, capacity utilization.
charts.avoidWhen: Multiple metrics or frequently changing data.

charts.name: Bullet Chart
charts.whenToUse: Displaying performance vs target with qualitative ranges.
charts.dataNeeded: Actual value + target value + 2-3 range thresholds.
charts.bestFor: Sales target tracking, scorecards, budget/SLA compliance.
charts.avoidWhen: Audience unfamiliar with the format.

charts.name: Progress Bar Chart
charts.whenToUse: Showing percentage completion toward goals for multiple items.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension + 1 numeric measure (percentage 0-100).
charts.bestFor: Project milestone completion, sales goal tracking, adoption rates.
charts.avoidWhen: Values are not naturally percentage-based.

charts.name: Sparkline
charts.whenToUse: Inline micro-trend alongside a headline metric.
charts.dataNeeded: 1 numeric measure across >=5 time points.
charts.bestFor: Dashboard tables with trend indicators.
charts.avoidWhen: Detailed trend analysis needed.

charts.name: Data Table
charts.whenToUse: When users need to read/compare individual records with precision.
charts.dataNeeded: Any structured dataset with 2-10 columns; under 200 rows.
charts.bestFor: Claims detail, policy list, transaction history, audit exports.
charts.avoidWhen: Patterns/trends matter more than individual values.

charts.name: Matrix / Pivot Table
charts.whenToUse: Comparing the same measure across two orthogonal dimensions.
charts.dataNeeded: 2 categorical dimensions + 1-2 numeric measures (pivot in SQL).
charts.bestFor: Sales by region x product, loss ratio by line x quarter.
charts.avoidWhen: Only one dimension varies, or >10 row/column values.

charts.name: Slicer - Checkbox List
charts.whenToUse: Interactive multi-select filter control (not a data visual).
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension.
charts.bestFor: Filtering by region, product line, status.
charts.avoidWhen: N/A - not emitted by the agent.

charts.name: Slicer - Dropdown Filter
charts.whenToUse: Interactive dropdown filter control (not a data visual).
charts.dataNeeded: 1 categorical dimension.
charts.bestFor: Dashboard-level filters.
charts.avoidWhen: N/A - not emitted by the agent.

charts.name: Date Range Slicer
charts.whenToUse: Interactive date-window filter control (not a data visual).
charts.dataNeeded: A date/datetime column.
charts.bestFor: Filtering a reporting time window.
charts.avoidWhen: N/A - not emitted by the agent.

charts.name: Choropleth / Filled Map
charts.whenToUse: Encoding a measure as fill color over geographic regions.
charts.dataNeeded: Geographic dimension + 1 numeric measure.
charts.bestFor: Market penetration by state, loss ratio by territory.
charts.avoidWhen: Regions vary wildly in size. No map renderer available - use horizontal-bar by region instead.

charts.name: Bubble / Point Map
charts.whenToUse: Plotting sized markers at geographic coordinates.
charts.dataNeeded: Lat/long + 1 numeric measure (size).
charts.bestFor: Branch revenue comparison, claims density by city.
charts.avoidWhen: Dense overlapping bubbles. No map renderer available.

charts.name: Tree / Org Chart
charts.whenToUse: Visualizing parent-child hierarchies as connected nodes.
charts.dataNeeded: Parent-child relationship data (id, parentId, label).
charts.bestFor: Org charts, account hierarchies, category trees.
charts.avoidWhen: Network-like relationships. No tree renderer - use treemap for hierarchy proportions.

charts.name: Sunburst Chart
charts.whenToUse: Multi-level part-to-whole breakdowns where depth and proportion matter.
charts.dataNeeded: 2-3 level categorical hierarchy + 1 numeric measure.
charts.bestFor: Revenue by division->department.
charts.avoidWhen: >3 levels. No sunburst renderer - use donut/treemap.

charts.name: Sankey Diagram
charts.whenToUse: Showing volume flow between source and target nodes.
charts.dataNeeded: Source-target pairs with a numeric flow value.
charts.bestFor: Customer journey, budget allocation, traffic attribution.
charts.avoidWhen: >12 nodes. No sankey renderer - summarize flows as stacked-bar.

charts.name: Gantt / Timeline Chart
charts.whenToUse: Planning/tracking project phases across a time horizon.
charts.dataNeeded: Task name + start date + end date.
charts.bestFor: Project roadmaps, SLA tracking.
charts.avoidWhen: No time component. No gantt renderer - use a table of tasks with dates.
